content.problemStatement:
Consider an N×MN \times MN×M binary matrix AAA, i.e. a grid consisting of NNN rows numbered 111 to NNN from top to bottom and MMM columns numbered 111 to MMM from left to right, whose cells take the value 000 or 111. Let (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) denote the cell on the iii-th row and jjj-th column.

The matrix is said to be special if for **every** cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), the following condition holds simultaneously:


Ai,j=1A_{i, j} = 1Ai,j​=1 if and only if the sum of row iii is equal to the sum of column jjj. More formally, Ai,j=1A_{i, j} = 1Ai,j​=1 iff ∑k=1MAi,k=∑k=1NAk,j\displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^{M} A_{i, k} = \sum_{k=1}^{N} A_{k, j}k=1∑M​Ai,k​=k=1∑N​Ak,j​
The specialty of a special matrix is defined to be the sum of values of all cells in the matrix. Given NNN and MMM, find the minimum specialty of a special matrix among all special binary matrices consisting of NNN rows and MMM columns.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing NNN and MMM — the number of rows and the number of columns.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum specialty of a special matrix among all special binary matrices consisting of NNN rows and MMM columns.
